sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Willdan Group stock hits all-time high at 135.27 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Willdan Group's stock reached an all-time high of $135.27, marking a 259% increase over the past year. This growth follows strong Q3 2025 financial results, the acquisition of Compass Municipal Advisors, and a significant $97 million contract with Alameda County for energy upgrades. Wedbush also raised its price target for the company to $145 with an Outperform rating.

feed.title: IBM’s A3 ratings affirmed by Moody’s, outlook changed to stable
feed.summary: Moody's Ratings has affirmed International Business Machines Corporation's (NYSE:IBM) A3 senior unsecured rating and changed its outlook from negative to stable, citing strong operating performance in 2025. The agency expects IBM's revenue and EBITDA growth to exceed 5% and 12% respectively in 2026, with gross leverage projected to decline. IBM's strong liquidity and focus on acquisitions for hybrid cloud growth are also key factors in the stable outlook.

feed.title: LG Electronics upgraded to Baa1 by Moody’s on improved metrics
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has upgraded LG Electronics Inc.'s issuer and senior unsecured ratings to Baa1 from Baa2, with a stable outlook. This upgrade reflects significant improvements in LG Display’s operating performance and reduced debt leverage, minimizing the financial support needed from LG Electronics. Both LG Display and LG Electronics have shown improved debt-to-EBITDA ratios, supported by debt reductions, asset sales, and a strategic shift towards high-margin OLED products.

feed.title: U.S. Bank CEO Kedia named chair
feed.summary: U.S. Bank CEO Gunjan Kedia will become the chair, consolidating the CEO and chair roles, a move that aligns U.S. Bank with all other eight U.S.-based global systemically important banks. Kedia's predecessor, Andy Cecere, is retiring from the board. Roland Hernandez will remain the lead independent director, a role often maintained to balance the power of a combined CEO-chair.

feed.title: Wolfe Research Adjusts Price Target on Host Hotels & Resorts to $21 From $19, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Wolfe Research has increased its price target for Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) to $21 from $19, while reaffirming an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated positive outlook from the firm regarding the specialized REIT. The article also lists several other recent analyst price target adjustments and ratings for Host Hotels & Resorts.

feed.title: Elevance Health posts Q4 gains
feed.summary: Elevance Health reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results meeting expectations but signaling future challenges, including a projected decrease in 2026 EPS. The company saw a 10% increase in Q4 operating revenue and strengthened its earnings power through pricing discipline, despite a decline in medical membership due to Medicaid attrition and higher medical cost trends. Elevance Health aims to return to at least 12% adjusted EPS growth by 2027 and returned $4.1 billion to shareholders in 2025.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Southwest Airlines Q4 2025 sees EPS beat, stock surges
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines reported Q4 2025 earnings with an EPS of $0.58, exceeding the forecast of $0.57, though revenue of $7.4 billion fell short of the $7.5 billion expectation. Despite the revenue miss, the company's stock surged 6.22% in premarket trading due to strong future guidance and operational achievements, including a projected 2026 adjusted EPS of at least $4. The call highlighted successful implementation of new initiatives like assigned seating, extra legroom, and expanded online presence, driving optimism for continued growth and profitability.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares stock slips as $1.75 billion note sale adds focus on Cadence deal financing
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares' stock slipped after the bank announced a $1.75 billion debt offering, drawing investor scrutiny on its balance-sheet strategy ahead of the Cadence Bank acquisition. The debt sale, comprising senior and subordinated notes, is intended to support Huntington's projected growth in 2026, which is partly tied to the Cadence merger. Investors are keenly watching the integration process and the impact of interest rate changes on bank margins.

feed.title: PulteGroup Sounds Cautious On Demand Even As Home Affordability Improves
feed.summary: PulteGroup (PHM) reported mixed fourth-quarter results, beating analyst expectations for adjusted earnings and revenue. Despite improved home affordability due to lower interest rates, the homebuilder noted that lagging consumer confidence continued to impact homebuyer demand. The company remains focused on asset management, cash flow generation, and developing a land pipeline to support future community count growth.

feed.title: Salesforce Hits Fresh 52-Week Low As Microsoft, SAP, ServiceNow Earnings Jolt Software Stocks
feed.summary: Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) shares have dropped to a new 52-week low following recent earnings reports from Microsoft, SAP, and ServiceNow, which have created a risk-off sentiment in the software sector. Concerns about cloud demand, deal momentum, and AI return on investment timelines have been reignited, negatively impacting Salesforce's stock which trades as a key enterprise software indicator. The company is experiencing a bearish trend, with its stock trading significantly below its moving averages, and is expected to report its next earnings on February 25th.

feed.title: Is Corteva (CTVA) Pricing Fully Reflects Food Security Tailwinds And Recent Share Gains
feed.summary: Corteva's stock performance has shown significant gains across various periods, yet a valuation analysis by Simply Wall St suggests it may be overvalued. Both a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and a Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison indicate that Corteva is trading at a premium, with the DCF model pointing to a 15.4% overvaluation and the P/E ratio exceeding industry and peer averages as well as Simply Wall St's proprietary Fair Ratio. Investors are encouraged to use "Narratives" to incorporate their own views and future expectations into valuation models to make informed decisions.

feed.title: Assessing Crown Castle (CCI) Valuation After Analyst Revisions Following DISH Wireless Default
feed.summary: Crown Castle (CCI) is undergoing a re-evaluation by analysts following DISH Wireless's default on payment obligations, prompting KeyBanc to adjust its models. Despite recent share price declines, CCI is trading at a significant discount according to Simply Wall St's narrative, driven by its strategic decision to sell its fiber segment and focus on being a pure-play U.S. tower company. The undervaluation is based on expectations of improved operational excellence, customer service, and profitability, supported by consistent growth in U.S. mobile data demand and 5G deployments.

feed.title: Microchip Technology (MCHP) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Microchip Technology (MCHP) is expected to report a significant year-over-year increase in earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company has a Zacks Rank #1 and a positive Earnings ESP of +2.18%, suggesting a high probability of beating consensus EPS estimates. Investors are advised to consider these factors, along with other market conditions, before its earnings release on February 5th.

feed.title: Affiliated Managers Group (AMG) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Q4 Release
feed.summary: Affiliated Managers Group (AMG) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and revenues for Q4 2025. While the Zacks Consensus Estimate predicts earnings of $8.75 per share, a slight downward revision has occurred. The company's Earnings ESP of -0.17% combined with a Zacks Rank #3 makes it difficult to definitively predict an earnings beat, despite a strong history of exceeding EPS estimates.

feed.title: Inside Franklin Templeton’s AI hub that cuts prep time and boosts client talks
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton has launched its "Intelligence Hub," an AI-driven distribution platform powered by Microsoft Azure, aimed at enhancing client engagement and productivity. This initiative builds on a 2024 collaboration with Microsoft to integrate advanced AI across workflows, and early pilots have shown significant improvements in preparation time and valuable client interactions. The platform centralizes data, automates tasks like list generation and meeting prep, and leverages Microsoft Dynamics 365 to deliver personalized, insight-driven experiences to clients and partners.

feed.title: Cardinal Health (CAH) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: Cardinal Health (CAH) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company's Zacks Earnings ESP of +1.77% combined with a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy) suggests a high probability of an earnings beat. Investors will be watching the actual results, expected February 5, to see their impact on the stock price.

feed.title: Analysts Estimate Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) to Report a Decline in Earnings: What to Look Out for
feed.summary: Analysts expect Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) to report a year-over-year decline in earnings and lower revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company's upcoming earnings report on February 5th will reveal how its actual results compare to the consensus estimates of $1.46 per share and $12.25 billion in revenue, representing a decrease from the prior year. Despite a strong history of beating EPS estimates over the last four quarters, the current Zacks Earnings ESP suggests a potential miss due to recent bearish revisions.

feed.title: Brinker Stock Rises as Chili’s Powers Earnings Beat, Higher Guidance
feed.summary: Brinker International, owner of Chili's Grill & Bar and Maggiano's Little Italy, announced better-than-expected quarterly earnings, leading to a temporary increase in its stock price. The positive results were largely driven by the strong performance of its Chili's brand. The company also provided higher guidance, suggesting continued optimism for its financial outlook.

feed.title: Fortinet (FTNT) Reports Next Week: What You Should Expect
feed.summary: Fortinet (FTNT) is projected to report flat earnings but higher revenues for the quarter ending December 2025. The company's upcoming earnings report on February 5th will reveal how actual results compare to the consensus estimates of $0.74 EPS and $1.86 billion in revenue. Based on the Zacks Earnings ESP and a Zacks Rank of #3, it is difficult to conclusively predict an earnings beat.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Lowered Diamondback Energy, Inc. (FANG) Target to $171 And Maintained Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for Diamondback Energy, Inc. (FANG) to $171 from $183, while maintaining an Overweight rating, citing updated oil price assumptions for 2026-27 and expectations of weaker cash flow. This adjustment follows a similar action by Mizuho, which also slightly reduced its target to $194 ahead of FANG's fourth-quarter earnings report. Diamondback Energy is an independent oil and natural gas company focused on the Permian Basin.

feed.title: Newmont Goldcorp stock hits all-time high at 134.33 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Newmont Goldcorp's stock reached an all-time high of $134.33, demonstrating a 222.64% one-year return and strong investor confidence within the Metals & Mining industry. The company, valued at $145.57 billion with a "GREAT" financial health rating, is capitalizing on rising gold demand despite recent operational challenges like bushfire damage impacting its Boddington output and fluctuating analyst ratings and share sale agreements.

feed.title: STERIS plc announces $0.63 quarterly dividend payable in March By Investing.com
feed.summary: STERIS plc (NYSE:STE) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share, payable on March 19, 2026. This extends the company's 21-year streak of dividend payments, showcasing an annual yield of 0.96% and 10.53% dividend growth over the last year. The healthcare and life sciences products provider, with a market capitalization of $25.57 billion, maintains a "GREAT" financial health score according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams (NYSE:SHW) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.06 EPS
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams (NYSE:SHW) announced its quarterly earnings, surpassing analyst estimates with an EPS of $2.23 against a consensus of $2.17. The company also reported revenues of $5.60 billion, slightly above the $5.57 billion estimate. Institutional investors hold a significant portion of its shares, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock.

feed.title: Altria earnings fall short amid lower cigarette sales and competition for nicotine products
feed.summary: Altria reported flat earnings, falling short of Wall Street expectations, primarily due to declining cigarette sales and intense competition in the market for newer nicotine products like pouches. The company is struggling to gain market share in e-cigarettes and nicotine pouches, with its "on! Plus" brand losing ground to competitors like Philip Morris International's Zyn. Altria's efforts to diversify face setbacks, including a $1.3 billion charge on its NJOY vaping business following a patent infringement ruling.

feed.title: IntercontinentalExchange (ICE) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: IntercontinentalExchange (ICE) is projected to report increased earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ending December 2025. While the Zacks Consensus Estimate shows a positive outlook and recent EPS revisions are slightly higher, the Most Accurate Estimate for ICE is lower, resulting in a negative Earnings ESP. Given this, combined with a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), it's difficult to conclusively predict an earnings beat, despite the company's strong history of exceeding EPS expectations in recent quarters.

feed.title: Legend Biotech stock hits 52-week low at $18.07 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Legend Biotech (LEGN) stock has reached a new 52-week low of $18.07, representing a 49.61% decline over the past year and a 56% drop in the last six months due to broader biotech challenges and an earnings miss for its CARVYKTI product. Despite this, the company maintains a strong financial position with more cash than debt and significant revenue growth, and analysts still see considerable upside potential, with some reiterating Buy ratings based on upcoming clinical milestones and regulatory developments.

feed.title: Mid-America $53M Settlement Lands in Sweeping Rent-Fixing MDL
feed.summary: Mid-America Apartment Communities Inc. has agreed to a $53 million settlement in multidistrict antitrust litigation, addressing claims of conspiring to fix apartment rents using RealPage Inc.'s pricing software. The settlement funds will resolve claims from a certified class of multifamily renters who allege that the software facilitated coordinated rent increases among landlords. This agreement marks a significant development in a case impacting the national rental housing industry.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ALERT Bernstein Liebhard LLP Announces A
feed.summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP has announced a securities fraud class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) on behalf of investors who purchased shares between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that F5 made misrepresentations regarding its security capabilities. Shareholders are encouraged to join the lawsuit, with a lead plaintiff deadline of February 17, 2026.

feed.title: Valero Energy says it will buy Venezuelan crude from three sellers
feed.summary: Valero Energy announced it will purchase Venezuelan crude from three authorized sellers, expecting it to constitute a significant portion of its heavy crude supply in February and March. The company projects its capacity to process Venezuelan crude will exceed 240,000 barrels per day. This move is anticipated to benefit U.S. refiners following efforts to boost Venezuela's oil output.

feed.title: Uniphore Named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Customer Data Platforms
feed.summary: Uniphore, the Business AI company, has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Customer Data Platforms, specifically for its Completeness of Vision and Ability to Execute. The company emphasizes its data warehouse-native, zero-copy architecture that integrates with existing platforms like Snowflake and Databricks, reducing costs and accelerating time to value. Uniphore's approach focuses on enterprise-wide, real-time decision-making and intelligence, with a platform designed for speed, precision, and scalability in personalization.

feed.title: U.S. vaccine hesitancy could impact investment
feed.summary: Growing vaccine hesitancy in the U.S. is expected to deter venture capital investment in vaccine startups and could reduce valuations for private equity-backed clinical research organizations. This trend is already impacting major pharmaceutical companies like Moderna, which is halting new infectious disease vaccine studies due to concerns about market access and ROI. Despite this, some investors believe the disruption will be temporary, anticipating a shift in public opinion as preventable diseases resurface.

feed.title: IBM, Royal Caribbean Cruises, Lockheed Martin, AT&T And Other Big Stocks Moving Higher On Thursday
feed.summary: Several major stocks, including IBM, Royal Caribbean Cruises, Lockheed Martin, and AT&T, saw significant gains on Thursday. Many of these companies reported better-than-expected quarterly financial results and issued optimistic guidance for the upcoming year. Despite an overall market downturn with the Nasdaq Composite falling, these individual stocks outperformed, driven by strong earnings and positive outlooks.

feed.title: Cantor Fitzgerald reiterates Overweight rating on Boston Properties stock
feed.summary: Cantor Fitzgerald reiterated its "Overweight" rating and $79.00 price target for Boston Properties (NYSE:BXP) despite an earnings miss in Q4 2025 due to higher G&A expenses. The firm highlighted the real estate investment trust's accelerating leasing activity and expected FFO growth, driven by office "flight-to-quality" trends. Boston Properties also offers an attractive 4.36% dividend yield and is projected to reach 91% office occupancy by the end of 2027.

feed.title: AT&T Earnings: Competition Remains Intense, but Management’s Outlook Points to Stability
feed.summary: AT&T's fourth-quarter revenue increased by 3.6%, driven largely by higher smartphone sales, and the company projects consistent service revenue growth, improving margins, and free cash flow generation through 2028. While it's gaining postpaid phone customers, increased revenue growth reflects intense wireless competition rather than fundamental business improvements, as customer churn remains high. Morningstar maintains its $26 fair value estimate, noting management's confidence in stable wireless competition and reduced future spectrum investment needs.

feed.title: Nvidia, Amazon, Microsoft in Talks for $60B OpenAI Investment
feed.summary: Nvidia, Amazon, and Microsoft are reportedly in preliminary discussions to invest a combined $60 billion in OpenAI, aiming to support the significant computational needs of next-generation AI models. This potential capital injection could elevate OpenAI's valuation to around $830 billion and is part of a broader funding effort potentially reaching $100 billion, including a SoftBank investment. However, some analysts express concerns about a potential AI bubble due to the circular nature of these deals, where funding from tech giants is often reinvested into their chip and cloud services.

feed.title: Parker Hannifin Raises Outlook On Aerospace Momentum
feed.summary: Parker Hannifin (NYSE: PH) reported strong Q2 fiscal 2026 results, surpassing analyst expectations for earnings and revenue, driven by robust aerospace demand and industrial market recovery. The company raised its full-year EPS and sales guidance, citing record backlog and significant growth in its Aerospace Systems segment. Shares reacted positively, trading higher after the announcement.

feed.title: United Rentals Q4 2025 slides: revenue growth slows, stock drops on missed forecasts
feed.summary: United Rentals (NYSE:URI) reported Q4 2025 revenue of $4.21 billion and adjusted EPS of $11.09, both missing analyst expectations, leading to an 11.32% drop in its stock price. Despite slower growth in Q4, the company achieved full-year 2025 revenue of $16.1 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $7.3 billion. United Rentals maintains a strong balance sheet, generates substantial free cash flow, and focuses on strategic initiatives like expanding its specialty segment and investing in technology, while also increasing its dividend and planning share repurchases for 2026.

feed.title: March 13th Options Now Available For Gilead Sciences (GILD)
feed.summary: New options contracts for Gilead Sciences (GILD) with a March 13th expiration date are now available. The article highlights a put option at a $135.00 strike price, offering a potential 1.71% return (14.54% annualized) if it expires worthless, and a covered call option at a $141.00 strike price, which could yield a 3.49% total return if assigned. It details the potential benefits and risks of these options strategies for investors.

feed.title: March 13th Options Now Available For CSX
feed.summary: New options contracts for CSX Corp (CSX) expiring on March 13th have become available. BNK Invest's YieldBoost formula highlights specific put and call options: a $37.00 strike put with a 50-cent bid providing a potential 1.35% return if it expires worthless, and a $38.00 strike call also with a 50-cent bid, offering a potential 2.39% return for a covered call strategy. The article details potential returns and risks for investors utilizing these options.

feed.title: March 13th Options Now Available For Ross Stores (ROST)
feed.summary: New options contracts for Ross Stores (ROST) with a March 13th expiration date are now available. Stock Options Channel's YieldBoost formula has identified interesting put and call contracts. A $160.00 strike price put contract offers a potential cost basis of $159.50 if an investor sells-to-open, while a $190.00 strike price call contract as a covered call offers a 4.69% total return if the stock is called away.

feed.title: March 13th Options Now Available For Cadence Design Systems (CDNS)
feed.summary: New options for Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) with a March 13th expiration are now available. BNK Invest has identified a put contract at a $295.00 strike price and a call contract at a $310.00 strike price, both offering potential YieldBoost returns for investors. The article details strategies for using these options, including selling-to-open put contracts and covered calls, alongside analysis of their probability of expiring worthless and implied volatilities.

feed.title: Align Technology (ALGN) Just Flashed Golden Cross Signal: Do You Buy?
feed.summary: Align Technology (ALGN) has recently shown a "golden cross" signal, where its 50-day simple moving average crossed above its 200-day simple moving average, indicating a potential bullish breakout. The company also holds a #2 (Buy) Zacks Rank with a positive earnings outlook, reinforcing the bullish case. Investors might consider ALGN for future gains due to its technical indicators and favorable earnings estimate revisions.

feed.title: Lubbock's Beauty Scene Could Expand With A New Ulta Store In 2026
feed.summary: Lubbock may soon get a second Ulta Beauty store, with development trackers suggesting a new location in the Commons South area. Although Ulta has not officially announced it, this expansion would cater to the city's growing retail demands, especially in South Lubbock, offering more convenience and access to beauty products and services. Construction could potentially begin in 2026, with an opening later the same year.

feed.title: Here's Why Paccar (PCAR) is a Strong Momentum Stock
feed.summary: Paccar (PCAR) is identified as a strong momentum stock based on Zacks' Style Scores. The article explains the methodology behind the Zacks Rank and Style Scores (Value, Growth, Momentum, and VGM), emphasizing how combining a high Zacks Rank with strong Style Scores can lead to better investment decisions. PCAR holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) but has an "A" Momentum Style Score and a "B" VGM Score, making it an attractive option for momentum investors.

feed.title: Stifel Raises Price Target for Lam Research (LRCX) to $280 | LRC
feed.summary: Stifel has reaffirmed its "Buy" rating for Lam Research (LRCX) and increased its price target from $250 to $280, signaling an optimistic future outlook. This follows similar bullish adjustments from other analysts including Citigroup, Deutsche Bank, Needham, and UBS, reinforcing confidence in the company's semiconductor sector position. Despite the positive analyst sentiment, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside for LRCX's GF Value compared to its current price.

feed.title: Costco vs. Dollar General: Which Discount Retailer Is the Better Buy?
feed.summary: This article compares Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) and Dollar General Corporation (DG), two prominent discount retailers, to determine which offers a better investment opportunity. While Costco's robust membership model and global expansion plans are highlighted, its elevated valuation is a concern. Dollar General is presented as a more compelling choice due to its ongoing operational reset, store upgrades, margin recovery, and expanding omnichannel presence, supported by a "Strong Buy" Zacks Rank.

feed.title: Lee Enterprises Schedules Audio Webcast and Conference Call for Preliminary Q1 Results on February 10, 2026
feed.summary: Lee Enterprises (NASDAQ: LEE) will host an audio webcast and conference call on February 10, 2026, at 9 a.m. Central Time to discuss preliminary financial results for its quarter ending December 28, 2025. The company, a major subscription and advertising platform, will issue a news release with these results prior to the market opening on that day. Interested parties can access the live webcast or register for a dial-in number to participate, with a replay available afterward.

feed.title: Recycling possible for PS petri dishes, other lab equipment
feed.summary: A feasibility study conducted by Becton, Dickinson & Co. and Envetec Sustainable Technologies indicates that plastic petri dishes and other laboratory equipment made from polystyrene (PS) could be recycled. This research explores a circular lifecycle for these widely used lab items, addressing the environmental impact of single-use plastics in scientific settings.

feed.title: Here's Why ResMed (RMD) is a Strong Momentum Stock
feed.summary: ResMed (RMD) has been identified as a strong momentum stock according to Zacks Style Scores, boasting a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy) and a VGM Score of A. The company, a major player in sleep-disordered breathing and respiratory care, has seen its shares rise 7% in the past four weeks, with analysts revising earnings estimates upwards for fiscal 2026. This indicates a promising outlook for investors seeking momentum-driven stocks.

feed.title: How Phillips 66's Integrated Model Enhances Profitability & Resilience
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) is well-positioned to benefit from falling crude prices, which reduce input costs for its refining operations and boost profitability. The company's diversified business model, spanning midstream, chemicals, and renewables, provides earnings stability, allowing it to sustain profitability even during weaker refining environments. PSX also focuses on divesting non-core assets to invest in high-return opportunities and enhance shareholder returns.
